title: identity — published macros
description: Two macros. `Str::password_strength` scores a plaintext password against the current policy without persisting anything (useful for the SPA's live strength indicator). `Blueprint::identity_fields` is the migration shorthand for the credential + audit columns that every Identity-shaped table carries.
namespace_note: Blueprint macros extend `Illuminate\Database\Schema\Blueprint`. Str macros extend `Illuminate\Support\Str`. Both are Laravel core classes.

macros.name: Str::password_strength

returns.breach_count: int | null (only set when breach check ran; null on offline mode)

macros.description: Score-without-side-effects. Consumers: (a) SPA password field renders a live strength indicator; (b) admin tooling grades an existing hash's likely-strength retrospectively; (c) breach-alert workflows re-check periodically. Does NOT hash the password — works on plaintext only. If `$identityId` is passed AND config('identity.password.history_size') > 0, also checks against the identity's password history and adds `in_password_history` to failed_requirements.

macros.name: Blueprint::identity_fields

columns.name: email
columns.description: PostgreSQL CITEXT for case-insensitive UNIQUE. On non-Postgres engines the macro falls back to `string` + a functional index on `LOWER(email)`.

columns.name: mfa_secret_encrypted
columns.description: KMS-envelope-encrypted TOTP secret. Never plaintext at rest.

columns.name: mfa_recovery_codes_hashed
columns.description: Array of bcrypt-hashed recovery codes. NULL = MFA not enrolled OR no codes issued.

columns.name: email_verification_token_hash
columns.description: SHA-256 hash of the outstanding verification token (plaintext never stored).

columns.name: password_history_hashed
columns.description: Rolling window of prior password hashes; oldest evicted at write time to respect history_size.

columns.name: dob
columns.description: For age-gate policy. Nullable because a platform-provisioned Identity (SSO test tenant) may lack dob — compliance-floor evaluation treats null as `undetermined = refuse`.

indexes.description: Partial unique index so soft-deleted identities can be replaced by fresh sign-ups on the same email during the GDPR hold-period. (Debatable — configurable via `identity.retention.replace_erased_identities`; default true.)

macros.description: Idempotent migration shorthand. Adds the credential + audit columns that appear on every Identity-shaped table. Should be called ONCE per table — second call fails with a clear duplicate-column error.
macros.boot_order_note: Registered by `IdentityServiceProvider::boot()`. Because module priority is 15 (below Wave 2+ modules), every downstream migration that would use this macro (e.g. `platform_users` in user module priority 18) sees it available.
